LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENT AND JUDICIAL ECONOMY SUPPORT THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF EUGENE SORIANO HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY ALLOWING THE AMENDMENT OR THAT ARXAN HAS ACTED IN "BAD FAITH." ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S DEMURRER TO ALL TEN CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF EUGENE SORIANO IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL TEN CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT AND THE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE SEPARATION AGREEMENT DO NOT NECESSARILY SHOW THAT ANY OR ALL OF MR. SORIANO'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE RELEASE LANGUAGE IN THE SEPARATION AGREEMENT.(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Arxan Technologies, Inc.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. The liberal policy in favor of amendment and judicial economy support the motion. Plaintiff Eugene Soriano has not shown that he would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment or that Arxan has acted in "bad faith."

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Set For Trial On Aug-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Zev Hardman, Esq. 510730-1706 of the Hardman Law for Plaintiff.
Tyler Brown, Esq. 415-394-9400 of Jackson Lewis for Defendant ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues duress. The Court strikes the word economic from the ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Arxan Technologies, Inc's demurrer to all ten causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Eugene Soriano is overruled as to all ten causes of action. The face of the complaint and the judicially noticeable separation agreement do not necessarily show that any or all of Mr. Soriano's claims are barred by the release language in the separation agreement. Liberally construed, Mr. Soriano's allegations are sufficient to support duress as grounds for rescission of the separation agreement. "The question of duress. . .is a factual question; the existence of duress always depends upon the circumstances" and it requires an examination of "the state of mind induced thereby in the victim. The means used to produce that condition, the age, sex, state of health, and mental characteristics of the alleged injured party, are all evidentiary, merely, of the ultimate fact in issue, of whether such person was bereft of the free exercise of his will power." (Philippine Export & Foreign Loan Guarantee Corp. v. Chuidian (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1058, 1078 (emphasis in the original)). Liberally construed, the complaint alleges that at the time he signed the separation agreement Mr. Soriano was suffering from hyperglycemia and was severely distraught and traumatized by his abusive work environment, including the statement that Mr. Soriano was compelled to quit his employment notwithstanding that he had almost six months left on his oral agreement and the grabbing of Mr. Soriano's office equipment while he was being threatened to be physically dragged out of the building with the shouting of "do you want me to call security?"

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
